Non-Partisan Civic Education · South Africa

Think Inside
the Ballot Box

We teach primary school children about their constitutional rights through interactive workshops, election simulations, and characters they can actually talk to. Not politics. Just rights.

Meet the Characters Book a Workshop
Who We Are

A Youth-Led Movement for Civic Literacy

The People Project is a non-profit, non-partisan civic education initiative that brings South Africa's Constitution to life for children aged 11–12. We believe that when young people understand their rights, they grow into active, informed citizens.

Election Workshops at Libraries

High-energy 60-minute sessions with games, debates, and a real mock election — right in their community.

Online Learning Portal

Age-appropriate lesson plans and resources educators can access anytime, built with teachers in mind.

Social Media Civic Education

Young South Africans ask real questions about the Constitution, business, and democracy — and get real expert answers.

100% Non-Partisan

Our six candidates are animals — not politicians. They each represent a constitutional right, not a political party. We teach children the process of democracy: how to vote, why it matters, and what the Bill of Rights actually gives them. No parties. No agendas. Just rights.

Rights, Not Parties
Our Approach

Three Ways We
Reach Young People

01

Library Workshops

Fun, fast-paced 60-minute sessions run at libraries and community spaces. Children play the Constitutional Headsup game, debate which rights matter most, then cast their vote in a real mock election with ballot sheets and a ballot box.

02

Online Learning Portal

A growing library of age-appropriate resources — lesson plans, activity sheets, and character profiles — freely available to educators across South Africa. Everything is aligned to the Bill of Rights and designed for classroom use.

03

AI Character Chats

Each of our six characters is powered by AI. Children can have real conversations with Zibi about education, ask Katlego about justice, or quiz Shoni on healthcare rights — learning the Constitution one message at a time.

6
Constitutional Rights
11–12
Target Age Group
3
Programme Tiers
100%
Non-Partisan
The Candidates

Six Rights.
Six Champions.

Each candidate stands for a right from South Africa's Bill of Rights. Click any character to open a chat — ask them anything about their right, the Constitution, or what it means for your life.

For Schools & Libraries

Book a Workshop

Our facilitators bring everything you need. All materials, activities, and stationery are supplied by the People Project team.

📚

Constitution 101 Presentation

A high-energy intro to the Bill of Rights covering all 6 constitutional rights, tailored for ages 11–12.

🃏

Constitutional Headsup Game

Groups of 4 rotate through cards pairing constitutional rights with their responsibilities.

🗳️

Mock Election

A real ballot box, personalised ballot sheets, and a thumb-stamp sticker for every voter. Democracy in action.

⏱️

60 Minutes. Maximum Fun.

Workshops run for one school period and fit into any school or library schedule.

Request a Workshop

Fill in your details and we'll be in touch within 48 hours to confirm a date.

Join the Movement

Get Involved

The People Project is powered by people who believe the next generation of South Africans deserves to know their rights.

Volunteer

Join our facilitator team. Help run workshops at libraries and schools in your community. No experience necessary — just energy and a belief in civic education.

Apply to Volunteer →

Donate

Every workshop costs money to run. Your donation funds ballot sheets, stickers, game cards, and the facilitators who make it happen. No amount is too small.

Make a Donation →

Partner With Us

Are you a school, library, foundation, or business that wants to bring civic education to your community? Let's talk about how we can work together.

Get in Touch →
